title: First Day at the Startup
textRussian: Алекс программист. Он работает в стартапе. Сегодня его первый день. Утром он приходит в офис. Его коллега Дмитрий говорит: «Привет! Добро пожаловать в команду!» Алекс счастлив. Он открывает ноутбук и видит код. Дмитрий показывает проект. «Это наш репозиторий», — говорит он. Алекс читает код. Код простой. Он понимает логику. День начинается хорошо.
textEnglish: Alex is a programmer. He works at a startup. Today is his first day. In the morning he arrives at the office. His colleague Dmitry says: "Hello! Welcome to the team!" Alex is happy. He opens his laptop and sees code. Dmitry shows him the project. "This is our repository," he says. Alex reads the code. The code is simple. He understands the logic. The day starts well.

vocabulary.context: The core profession. In Russian tech, commonly used interchangeably with 'разработчик' (developer).
Borrowed from English, widely used in Russian tech culture with the same meaning.
Common phrase for onboarding. 'Первый' (first) agrees with 'день' (day) in gender/case.
Neutral term for team member. Used in both formal and informal settings.
Direct borrowing from English 'notebook'. Essential dev workspace vocabulary.
Same as English. Masculine noun. 'Писать код' = to write code.
Technical term for code storage. Often shortened to 'репо' in casual speech.

exercises.question: Алекс работает в _______. Сегодня его первый _______.
exercises.explanation: The sentence means: 'Alex works at a startup. Today is his first day.' Note the prepositional case ending '-е' for 'стартап' after 'в'.

exercises.question: What does Dmitry show to Alex on the first day?
exercises.explanation: Dmitry says: 'Это наш репозиторий' (This is our repository).

exercises.question: Find the error: 'Алекс программисты. Он работает в офис.'
exercises.explanation: 'Программисты' is plural, should be 'программист' (singular). Also, after 'в' (in/at) we need prepositional case: 'в офисе' not 'в офис'.

exercises.question: Translate to Russian: 'My colleague is a programmer.'
exercises.explanation: Note: No article needed in Russian. 'Мой' (my) agrees with masculine 'коллега'.

pronunciation.pitfalls: Stress on the second 'o'. English speakers often stress the first syllable incorrectly.

storyArc: Introduction to the tech startup world. Alex begins his journey.
